commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces hardcoded handshake versions, adds persisted node-token startup reconnect, rewrites exec approval to a native Win32 prompt, updates the manifest, and adds regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: configure `EnableMcpServer=true`, `EnableNodeMode=false`, an active gateway record with a persisted node token, and no operator credential; the new branch will call `ConnectNodeOnlyAsync` and skip `TryStartLocalMcpOnlyNode()`. I did not run a Windows tray instance in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused automated repair can preserve the PR's intended reconnect behavior while fixing the settings/security regression with targeted tests.

target.title: fix(node): reconnect and report app version after restart
target.body:
## Summary
- replace hardcoded `1.0.0` Windows node/tray handshake version fields with `AppVersionInfo.Version`
- reconnect already-paired Windows nodes on startup using the persisted node device token instead of requiring bootstrap/shared credentials
- preserve credential precedence so startup does not downgrade a paired node from its device token back to bootstrap/shared tokens
- fix the native exec approval prompt loop so real button clicks complete the pending `system.run` approval
- add regression coverage for node-only reconnect, SSH tunnel startup, and startup fallback wiring

## Validation
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean on the branch after the earlier review fixes
- CI Build and Test run `26727321535` on `9173e4e`: repo-hygiene, test, e2etests, build win-x64, build win-arm64 all passed
- staged current x64 artifact on an Azure crabbox: `0.6.0-PullRequest613.736`, expected `NotSigned` for PR artifact
- node identity/version check on current artifact: same paired node/device identity, connected=true, pending=0, node version reports `0.6.0-PullRequest613.736`
- restart hardening checks on this branch: tray restart, Windows VM reboot + tray start, and Linux gateway restart all reconnected the same paired node/device with pending=0 and no credential downgrade observed
- exec approval interactive matrix on current artifact using real mouse input:
  - Deny returned policy denial instead of timing out
  - Allow once executed the command
  - Always allow executed the command and persisted an exact allow rule
  - Repeat of the always-allowed command executed without prompting
- restored the crabbox approval policy to its original `defaultAction=deny`, 22-rule state after testing

## Known Follow-Up
- Gateway `approvals set --node` accepted a stale `baseHash` after an `Always allow` prompt-created rule changed the node policy. Direct `node.invoke system.execApprovals.*` is intentionally blocked by gateway policy, so this compare-and-set enforcement gap appears to live in OpenClaw gateway `exec.approvals.node.*`, not this Windows tray PR.
- PR artifacts are unsigned. The Azure Trusted Signing release workflow remains the path for signed alpha/beta release artifacts.
target.author: vincentkoc
